hypothesis: 建立结构化实验日志格式后，后续迭代可以避免重复失败尝试并保留参数记忆。
change_summary: 新增实验日志文件与字段规范，作为未来所有 accepted/rejected 尝试的统一落点。
notes: 这是台账初始化记录，不是策略实验结果。后续每次策略尝试都应按本格式追加一行。

hypothesis: Trailing stops (high_water - N*ATR after profit trigger) outperform the 3.5x ATR fixed target across BULL/NEUTRAL/BEAR windows.
change_summary: Backtester Position class gained trailing-stop infrastructure (TRAIL_TRIGGER_ATR_MULT, TRAIL_OFFSET_ATR_MULT); kept off by default because 3 swept configs were strictly worse than baseline in every window.

delta_metrics.note: All 3 configs strictly worse than baseline in every window; see after_metrics.by_window_pnl_sharpe for cell-by-cell deltas.

rejection_reason: No config outperformed baseline in all 3 non-overlapping windows. trail 3.0/2.0 beat baseline on Oct25-Apr26 PnL but lost on Sharpe, and was strictly worse on Apr25-Oct25 and Oct24-Apr25 — violates AGENTS.md Gate 4 multi-window stability requirement.
next_retry_requires: Different parameter space (e.g. percent-based trails instead of ATR multiples)
Evidence that 3.5x ATR fixed target is wrong for the current regime mix
New window data in BEAR-heavy regime where trails may behave differently
notes: Infrastructure left in codebase with default-off constants for future sweeps.

hypothesis: A regime-gated passive SPY overlay (deploy idle cash into SPY when SPY 10-day momentum > threshold) captures market beta during ripping BULL windows without changing active signal decisions.
change_summary: Planned allocation-layer change. Rejected in planning without executing; the motivation was single-window (Apr25-Oct25 secondary) and AGENTS.md §6.1 blocks strategy tweaks while LLM gate is un-replayed.
component: quant/backtester.py (overlay state machine between exit-check and signal-entry, not executed)

market_regime_summary.secondary: BULL (ripping, +10% strategy vs +30% SPY — source of motivation)

delta_metrics.note: Never executed — rejected in planning per AGENTS.md §6.1.

rejection_reason: AGENTS.md §6.1 blocks strategy tweaks while LLM decisions are un-replayed and un-attributed (llm_gate_unreplayed=True in known_biases). AGENTS.md §2.1 flags single-window motivation as over-fitting. AGENTS.md §1.2 requires checking history before new iterations — this was the first plan written against AGENTS.md and it failed those gates.
next_retry_requires: LLM replay coverage >= 30 days so strategy-layer measurement is not confounded
At least one BEAR window in the acceptance gate (current 3 windows are all BULL variants)
Regime label (not just 10d momentum) to avoid deploying into dead-cat bounces
An intraday emergency-exit mechanism (10d momentum is lagging; sharp reversals eat drawdown)
notes: Rejected-before-execution record kept per AGENTS.md §6.2 so future agents do not rediscover and re-propose the same thing.

hypothesis: Wiring the backtester to replay LLM decisions from data/llm_prompt_resp_YYYYMMDD.json when present (default off) closes the §6.1 production-vs-backtest parity gap without degrading off-mode metrics, and produces the first §4.2-compliant LLM attribution bucket.
change_summary: New quant/llm_replay.py module; BacktestEngine gained --replay-llm flag; known_biases.llm_gate_unreplayed restructured from bool to dict{enabled,coverage_fraction,dates_covered,dates_missing_n}; result['llm_attribution'] added.

llm_metrics.llm_attribution_metric: llm_attribution bucket (coverage_fraction, signals_presented, signals_vetoed_by_llm, signals_passed_by_llm, veto_rate)
llm_metrics.note: Sample too small (5 response files over 124 trading days) for real alpha attribution; logging the mechanism is the deliverable, not the alpha claim.

notes: Off-mode numerics identical to baseline (code path is pure passthrough when replay_llm=False). On-mode attribution is statistically immaterial at n=5 covered days — value is the mechanism, not the current delta.

hypothesis: Turning known_biases.news_veto_unreplayed from a bare bool(True) into a structured coverage bucket (parallel to llm_gate_unreplayed's exp-004 upgrade) makes the news-archive parity gap auditable per-run without touching decision logic. Numerical strategy metrics MUST be identical to baseline by construction because no signal/entry/exit/sizing code path was modified.
change_summary: BacktestEngine.run() now per-day checks whether data/clean_trade_news_YYYYMMDD.json exists and surfaces the coverage as known_biases.news_veto_unreplayed = {archive_replay_enabled:False, archive_coverage_fraction, archive_dates_covered, archive_dates_missing_n}. test_backtester_emits_known_biases_and_integrity updated to match new schema.

known_biases.notes[0].old: news veto lives in filter.py (EVENT_KEYWORDS + T1_TITLE_KEYWORDS); no historical archive
known_biases.notes[0].new: news veto lives in filter.py (EVENT_KEYWORDS + T1_TITLE_KEYWORDS); clean_trade_news_YYYYMMDD.json archive coverage is reported but no replay engine exists yet

market_regime_summary.primary: BULL (slow-melt; same window as baseline exp-004)

after_metrics.note: Minor drift from market data refresh — 2026-04-17 OHLCV finalized after baseline was recorded. Structural metrics (trade_count=30, win_rate=60%, drawdown=4.59%) are identical; PnL/sharpe differ <2% due to end-of-day price data finalization.

delta_metrics.note: All delta attributable to yfinance price refresh for 2026-04-17 (last day of window), not code change. No signal/entry/exit/sizing path was modified. confirmed by: test_backtester_emits_known_biases_and_integrity PASSED; known_biases.news_veto_unreplayed is dict with archive_coverage_fraction=0.1048 (13/124 days).

llm_metrics.note: Measurement change only — no LLM prompt or boundary touched. Unlocks a future §4.2 attribution experiment where signals can be regressed against T1 news presence using the now-exposed archive_dates_covered list.

notes: Validated 2026-04-18. pytest PASSED (test_backtester_emits_known_biases_and_integrity). Actual archive_coverage_fraction=0.1048 (13/124 trading days) — matches the predicted ~10%. 13 covered dates span 2026-01-23 to 2026-04-17. archive_dates_missing_n=111. Follow-up: exp-20260418-002 built news_replay.py module on top of this bucket.

hypothesis: Building news_replay.py (parallel to llm_replay.py) to apply T1-negative news veto in backtester --replay-news mode closes the §6.1 news-veto parity gap for covered dates. Off-mode metrics must be identical to baseline; on-mode measures the actual veto impact.
change_summary: New quant/news_replay.py module with _T1_NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS classifier, get_news_for_date(), apply_news_gate(). BacktestEngine gained replay_news parameter and --replay-news CLI flag. news_attribution bucket added to result (parallel to llm_attribution). known_biases.news_veto_unreplayed.archive_replay_enabled now reflects replay_news state.

delta_metrics.note: Off-mode: bit-identical to before_metrics (pure passthrough). On-mode: zero vetoes across 6 signals on 13 covered days → news_veto bias is NEGLIGIBLE for this window. The optimistic bias from un-replayed news veto is smaller than feared: quant signals and T1-negative news are anti-correlated (bullish quant signal rarely coincides with bearish T1 news).

notes: Key insight: 0 vetoes out of 6 signals on 13 covered days. This is expected — if a ticker has T1-negative news (earnings miss, guidance cut), the quant signal engine would likely NOT generate a bullish trend/breakout signal for it on that day (stock would be down, trend broken). The two filters are anti-correlated. This means the 'news veto bias' label overstates the actual gap — the bias exists in theory but may be near-zero in practice for pure quant signals. In 217 pytest tests pass. Establish true baseline EV score: 0.4875 (updated from 0.4954, which was stale from before market data refresh).

hypothesis: import_advice.py currently saves only investment_advice_YYYYMMDD.json (for forward_tester) but NOT llm_prompt_resp_YYYYMMDD.json (needed by backtester --replay-llm). The user must manually create the replay files. Fixing import_advice to also write the replay file will close this workflow gap and make each LLM import automatically contribute to the 30-day replay coverage target (P-LLM).
change_summary: import_advice.import_advice() now also writes llm_prompt_resp_YYYYMMDD.json alongside investment_advice_YYYYMMDD.json. Guard added: skips if file already exists (preserves manual files) or if response is a fake save-prompt acknowledgment. llm_replay.py docstring corrected. 3 new tests added.

import_advice.import_advice.new: also writes llm_prompt_resp_YYYYMMDD.json when real LLM response detected

delta_metrics.note: No change to backtest metrics — this is pure infrastructure plumbing. Each future import_advice run will now automatically contribute 1 day to the 30-day P-LLM coverage target without requiring a separate manual step. Key finding during investigation: Feb 14-20 investment_advice files were fake (save-prompt acknowledgments, not real LLM responses) — backfilling them as replay files would have incorrectly vetoed all signals on those dates. Guard prevents this class of error.

notes: Also fixed: llm_replay.py docstring previously claimed files were written by import_advice.py (false until this change). 223/223 tests pass. P-LLM progress: 5 trading days covered (Apr 13-17), 25 more needed for 30-day threshold.

hypothesis: lxml was not installed, causing yfinance get_earnings_dates() to silently fail for all tickers in the backtester. Installing lxml enables the earnings calendar and allows earnings_event_long to fire. This is the root cause of earnings_event_long showing 0 trades in prior backtests.
change_summary: Installed lxml==6.1.0 (missing dependency). Added known_biases.earnings_event_long_data_quality disclosure to backtester output. New finding: earnings_event_long fires 12 trades at 33% win rate (profit_factor=0.33) when positive_surprise_history=None. Also displaces A+B trades (MAX_POSITIONS cap): trend_long 13->11, breakout_long 17->15.

delta_metrics.note: EV degraded 46.8%. Gate 4 fails on strategy metrics. Carve-out applies: this is a data gap fix, not strategy change. Key findings: (1) C strategy at 33% win rate with partial data is genuinely bad; (2) C displaces A+B slots (A went 13->11, B went 17->15); (3) Combined effect explains most of EV decline. P-ERN is now highest priority to determine if C strategy improves with full data.

next_retry_requires: Accumulate daily earnings snapshots (P-ERN) for >=10 days to test if positive_surprise_history improves C strategy win rate above 50%
Re-evaluate earnings_event_long with full data before concluding strategy is viable
notes: A+B strategies alone: EV=0.4875 (excellent, 60% WR, PF>3). C strategy alone: EV contribution negative (33% WR, PF=0.33). New baseline including C: EV=0.2595. The displacement effect (C taking A+B slots) is a second-order negative. AGENTS.md baseline should be updated to EV=0.2595. 223/223 tests pass.

hypothesis: The repository already has earnings snapshot replay support in the backtester, but only quant/run.py persisted daily snapshots. Wiring the same structured snapshot write into every production entry point that fetches earnings data closes the P-ERN data accumulation gap without changing strategy logic.
change_summary: Added quant/earnings_snapshot.py as a single helper for structured daily earnings snapshot persistence, then wired it into quant/run.py, quant/run_quant.py, and quant/run_pipeline.py immediately after earnings data fetch. Added 2 tests covering schema filtering and idempotent same-day writes.

snapshot_writer.new: shared persist_earnings_snapshot() helper used by all 3 entry points

write_behavior.new: all entry points skip existing same-day file via shared helper

after_metrics.note: Backtester rerun on the baseline window remained effectively unchanged. Max drawdown drifted from 4.59% to 4.61%, within normal market data refresh noise.

delta_metrics.note: Gate 4 alpha thresholds are intentionally not the acceptance criterion here because this is a measurement/data-gap repair. The deliverable is future snapshot coverage, not immediate metric uplift on already-missing historical days.

next_retry_requires: Allow the production pipelines to run for multiple days so earnings snapshots accumulate in data/earnings_snapshot_YYYYMMDD.json
After snapshot coverage improves, re-evaluate earnings_event_long quality and slot displacement with historical replay
notes: This iteration changed one causal variable only: daily earnings snapshot persistence coverage. It does not widen LLM scope, add filters, or retune thresholds. Verification: targeted pytest run passed (7 tests), and the baseline-window backtester rerun reproduced EV=0.2595.

hypothesis: LLM 是否有选择地否决 C 策略（earnings_event_long）的坏信号。通过在 backtester.py 中添加 llm_attribution.by_strategy 策略分层，可以分别统计每个策略的 LLM 否决率。如果 LLM 对 C 策略的否决率高于 A/B 策略，则 LLM 有策略识别能力，正在主动保护系统。
change_summary: 在 backtester.py 中添加 per-strategy LLM veto 统计。_pre_gate 快照 + _after_tickers 对比计算每个策略的 presented/vetoed/passed/veto_rate。llm_attribution dict 新增 by_strategy 字段。新增 2 个测试（by_strategy 计数正确性 + 空目录返回空 dict）。

llm_attribution.by_strategy.new: dict per strategy: {presented, vetoed, passed, veto_rate}

delta_metrics.note: 关键发现：LLM 对 earnings_event_long 的否决率（83.3%）显著高于 breakout_long（50%）。5天样本太小无法结论性判断，但方向正确——LLM 确实在过滤 C 策略信号。6个月全窗口 --replay-llm 显示 1/124 天覆盖（仅 Apr 13 有信号到达 LLM gate，其余 Apr 14-17 因持仓满额未产生新信号），EV 从 0.2595 → 0.2763（+6.5%）。

next_retry_requires: 需要积累更多 LLM 数据（当前 5 天，目标 30 天）以得出统计显著的结论
P-ERN: 等待 earnings_snapshot 数据积累，验证 positive_surprise_history 是否改善 C 策略胜率
当 LLM 覆盖率 >= 30 天后，运行 --replay-llm 并对比 by_strategy veto_rate 是否稳定
notes: 231/231 tests pass. by_strategy 分层功能已实现并测试。AGENTS.md §一 门槛已为 5 天：该实验满足 5 天覆盖要求，完成了首次 P-LLM 归因运行。下一步：继续积累 LLM 和 earnings 快照数据。

hypothesis: ATR_TARGET_MULT=3.5 may be premature. All 18 target-hit trades were 100% wins (avg +7.76% each). If winners naturally run past 3.5x ATR, raising the multiplier captures more upside. If price barely reaches target, raising it converts winners to EOB/stops and loses money.
change_summary: Added atr_target_mult parameter to enrich_signal_with_risk() and enrich_signals() in risk_engine.py. Added ATR_TARGET_MULT to DEFAULT_CONFIG in backtester.py and wired it through. Swept ATR_TARGET_MULT=[3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0] across 3 non-overlapping 6-month windows (same entry/stop/C-strategy baseline). ATR_TARGET_MULT=4.0 best in primary window but degrades in secondary+tertiary.

delta_metrics.note: W1 (primary) ATR_TARGET_MULT=4.0 best: +28.8% PnL (+$4206 vs baseline 3.5), +18.6% Sharpe (2.17 vs 1.83). However: W2 best is 3.0 (+13.5% vs 3.5), W3 all unprofitable (4.0 least-bad). No single multiplier improves in >=2/3 windows per Gate 4.

rejection_reason: AGENTS.md Gate 4 multi-window stability: ATR_TARGET_MULT=4.0 (best in W1) regresses W2 and leaves W3 unprofitable. No multiplier improves EV >10% in >=2/3 windows. Interpretation: 3.5x target is well-calibrated to primary BULL regime; exit alpha leak is NOT in target width. Likely source: regime-dependent exit (different target mult per BULL/NEUTRAL/BEAR).
next_retry_requires: Regime-aware exit (A6): test different target multipliers conditional on market_regime
Higher-quality BEAR-regime window data (W3 Oct24-Apr25 had structural losses across all multipliers)
Percent-based trails if returning to trailing stops (exp-002 ATR-multiple trails failed)
notes: Parametrization code remains in codebase; infrastructure is sound. Rejection is on alpha direction, not implementation quality. Exp-002 trailing-stop rejection also listed target-width concerns in next_retry_requires; this experiment confirms current target is not the alpha source.

hypothesis: Cross-window instability may come from slot competition, not entry logic. If incomplete-data earnings_event_long signals are demoted in same-day allocation ranking, A/B strategies should reclaim scarce slots and improve expected_value_score without changing entry/exit rules.
change_summary: Added a shared ranking helper that demoted earnings_event_long when positive_surprise_history was missing, while leaving all other signals in existing confidence order. Wired the ranking into backtester.py, run.py, run_quant.py, and run_pipeline.py before slot competition. Added 2 focused tests for the ranking behavior, then reverted the code after Gate 4 failure.

allocation_ranking.new: incomplete earnings_event_long demoted below all non-penalized signals; others remain confidence ordered

rejection_reason: Gate 4 failed immediately in the primary window. The simple allocation demotion did not reduce earnings_event_long trade count at all (12 -> 12), but it did replace one strong trend trade with a weaker trend trade mix, lowering EV 19.5% and Sharpe materially. Conclusion: the instability source is not 'incomplete C should always be ranked behind A/B' as a universal rule.
next_retry_requires: If revisiting ranking, use a richer and explicitly testable score than a binary strategy penalty
Need attribution that shows actual same-day slot collisions before changing allocation again
Do not retry the same 'demote incomplete earnings' heuristic without new evidence that those specific slot collisions are the loss source
notes: The implementation was fully rolled back after the failed backtest. Focused tests passed before rollback, so this rejection is about alpha direction, not coding quality.

hypothesis: Given exp-20260418-004 showed earnings_event_long at 33.3% win rate and PF=0.33 on partial data, the cleanest alpha-preserving move may be to disable C entirely until P-ERN data quality recovers. If C is the true drag, removing it should improve expected_value_score across multiple non-overlapping windows without changing any A/B entry or exit rules.
change_summary: Added a single-source ENABLED_STRATEGIES constant and wired it through backtester.py, run.py, run_quant.py, and run_pipeline.py. Default strategy set is now trend_long + breakout_long; earnings_event_long remains implemented but is disabled by default pending P-ERN recovery. Added focused tests for strategy gating and default config alignment.
component: quant/constants.py + quant/signal_engine.py + quant/backtester.py + quant/run.py + quant/run_quant.py + quant/run_pipeline.py + quant/test_quant.py

earnings_event_long.new: implemented but disabled by default until data quality improves

delta_metrics.note: EV improved in all 3 non-overlapping windows and the full window. Trade count fell because 12 low-quality C trades were removed, but win rate and Sharpe improved enough that the loss of count is acceptable under Gate 4.

next_retry_requires: Do not re-enable earnings_event_long by default until P-ERN provides enough snapshot coverage to test it with real eps_estimate / surprise-history inputs
When re-testing C, compare it as an explicit A/B/C toggle experiment across the same 3 windows
If new earnings data arrives, test whether C adds incremental EV without displacing stronger A/B opportunities
notes: This is a true single-variable alpha decision: no threshold tuning, no prompt change, no new filter. We are not deleting the earnings strategy; we are removing it from the default risk budget until its data quality is strong enough to justify inclusion.

hypothesis: A tradable regime-aware exit does not require predicting the 'true market regime'. If we use only entry-day observable index state (SPY/QQQ vs 200MA plus 10-day momentum) to smoothly widen or tighten the ATR target for A+B trades, the system may capture more upside in risk-on tapes without relying on hindsight labels.
change_summary: Added a documented regime-aware-exit design and implemented a backtester experiment that maps entry-day SPY/QQQ distance-from-200MA plus 10-day momentum into a smooth ATR target multiplier. The target width is bounded (3.0x to 4.5x ATR) and capped by regime (NEUTRAL <= 3.75x, BEAR <= 3.25x). Added audit fields to trades and a CLI flag `--regime-aware-exit` for reproducibility. Default behavior remains unchanged unless the flag/config is enabled.

target_mapping.new: entry-day smooth target in [3.0, 4.5] ATR, capped by regime

delta_metrics.note: North-star EV improved in all 3 non-overlapping windows and the full window. Primary-window EV rose 14.2%, Sharpe daily rose 0.20, and total return rose 1.1 pp. Drawdown increased modestly (4.59% -> 4.80%) but remained well inside constraints.

next_retry_requires: Before promoting this to production defaults, compare the same regime-aware exit against at least one additional older non-overlapping window to make sure the benefit is not specific to the current sample mix
If promoting live, port the same entry-day target mapping into run.py / run_quant.py so production and backtest stay aligned
If the next experiment extends this, keep the causal variable clean: test in-trade regime updates separately from entry-day target mapping
notes: This experiment deliberately avoids hindsight regime labels. The exit template is frozen at entry using only same-day observable index inputs. Current code keeps the feature opt-in via `config={"REGIME_AWARE_EXIT": True}` or CLI `--regime-aware-exit`; the accepted result is the alpha direction, not yet a forced production default.

hypothesis: Once a backtested alpha improvement is accepted, leaving production on the old exit logic creates a research/production mismatch and forfeits the measured edge. Porting the same shared regime-aware exit profile into the daily entry points should preserve parity without changing the already-validated formula.
change_summary: Extracted the entry-day regime-aware exit profile into a shared module (`quant/regime_exit.py`) and wired it into `run.py`, `run_quant.py`, and `run_pipeline.py`. Production signals now use the same target-width mapping as the backtester by default, and exported signals carry `target_mult_used`, `regime_exit_bucket`, and `regime_exit_score` for auditability. Also promoted `REGIME_AWARE_EXIT=True` to constants as the new default.
component: quant/regime_exit.py + quant/constants.py + quant/backtester.py + quant/run.py + quant/run_quant.py + quant/run_pipeline.py + quant/test_quant.py

REGIME_AWARE_EXIT.new: shared default on both backtest and production

shared_exit_logic.new: single compute_regime_exit_profile() module used across entry points

before_metrics.backtest_accepted_alpha: EV 0.5565 with regime-aware exit vs 0.4875 baseline

after_metrics.production_exit_logic: shared regime-aware exit profile enabled by default

delta_metrics.note: This is a parity deployment, not a new alpha search. The accepted alpha result remains exp-20260419-004; this change ensures daily runs now express that same logic instead of reverting to the old fixed target.

next_retry_requires: Monitor forward trades and compare realized exits against `target_mult_used` / `regime_exit_bucket`
If live behavior diverges, inspect production data quality rather than re-questioning the exit formula first
notes: Validation included targeted pytest coverage plus direct module import checks for the three production entry points. A sandbox py_compile attempt failed because the environment blocked __pycache__ writes, so import validation was used instead.

hypothesis: If the third-window weakness is mainly a pressure-environment problem, disabling `breakout_long` on pressure days should improve expected_value_score while preserving the rest of the A+B system.
change_summary: Added a backtester-only experiment that disabled `breakout_long` when the day-level environment looked hostile (`BEAR` regime or defensive entry-day exit profile), then compared it against the current A+B + regime-aware-exit baseline across 3 non-overlapping windows and the full window. The implementation was fully rolled back after the result proved null.

delta_metrics.note: The experiment produced zero change in every tested window because the pressure filter never fired on any breakout signals. It did not degrade the system, but it also did not test the intended causal mechanism.

rejection_reason: Rejected for null effect / wrong proxy. The broad market pressure proxy did not overlap with the actual failed breakout entries. In the third window, all three breakout trades entered on dates that the system still labeled `BULL` (NFLX 2026-03-02, AMZN 2026-04-10, AMD 2026-04-17). This means the true failure mode is not 'breakouts only fail in obvious BEAR/defensive tapes'; it is more likely a strategy-specific regime problem (follow-through quality) that broad index regime does not capture.
next_retry_requires: Do not retry broad `BEAR/defensive -> disable breakout` gating without a more strategy-specific regime proxy
Next candidate should target breakout follow-through directly (for example, a strategy-regime or quality proxy), not broad macro regime labels
Evaluate whether the LLM/news veto archive would have filtered the failed breakout dates before adding new hard-coded macro gates
notes: Implementation was rolled back immediately after the null result, per AGENTS.md Gate 4. This failed attempt was still valuable because it falsified the 'broad pressure regime' explanation for the third-window breakout weakness.

hypothesis: The remaining breakout fragility is not a broad-market pressure problem but a breakout-quality problem. Deep-recovery breakouts far below the 52-week high have weaker follow-through than true continuation breakouts. Adding a breakout-only hard gate that requires `pct_from_52w_high >= threshold` may improve expected_value_score without tightening unrelated strategies or altering exits.
change_summary: Added a single configurable breakout-only hard gate (`BREAKOUT_MAX_PULLBACK_FROM_52W_HIGH`) to `signal_engine.generate_signals()` / `strategy_b_breakout()`, exposed it through the backtester for sweep experiments, then promoted the accepted threshold into shared constants and all production entry points. The accepted threshold is -0.20, meaning breakout_long now rejects setups more than 20% below the 52-week high.
component: quant/signal_engine.py + quant/backtester.py + quant/constants.py + quant/run.py + quant/run_quant.py + quant/run_pipeline.py + quant/test_quant.py

scope.old: no breakout-specific hard gate on 52-week pullback depth

delta_metrics.note: The accepted threshold did not improve every window, but it did not regress window_1 or window_2 and materially improved the stress window while lifting the primary-window north-star metric. The tighter -0.05 gate overfit the sample: higher primary EV, but window_3 collapsed, so it was explicitly rejected.

llm_metrics.note: This experiment addresses a quantified breakout follow-through weakness. It does not reduce LLM scope and does not claim that LLM/news veto is unnecessary.

next_retry_requires: Do not tighten the gate beyond -0.20 without new multi-window evidence; -0.05 looked better in-sample but degraded the stress window materially
If revisiting breakout quality, test one strategy-specific proxy at a time (for example, above_200ma hard gate or TQS floor), not multiple breakout filters together
When enough LLM/news archive accumulates, check whether the veto layer independently flags the same deep-recovery breakout dates before adding more hard-coded filters
notes: Trade-level audit before the sweep showed the most suspicious losing breakouts were deep-recovery setups such as NFLX (pct_from_52w_high=-0.2824, above_200ma=False) and NVO (pct_from_52w_high=-0.3172). A paired same-data rerun was used for the final before/after metrics because vendor data refresh can cause absolute backtest levels to drift across separate downloads; the decision is based on within-batch deltas, not on raw point estimates from different fetch batches.

hypothesis: After filtering deep-recovery breakouts, remaining slot competition may still be leaving money on the table because breakout candidates with equal confidence are not ordered by breakout quality. Re-ranking only the `breakout_long` subsequence by `pct_from_52w_high` should improve slot selection without changing trend ordering, exits, or strategy enablement.
change_summary: Added a shared `rank_signals_for_allocation()` helper that preserves the exact relative order of non-breakout signals and only re-orders the `breakout_long` subsequence by `pct_from_52w_high` (closer to 52-week high first, then confidence). Wired the helper into backtester.py, run.py, run_quant.py, and run_pipeline.py behind a config flag, validated the experiment, then promoted `BREAKOUT_RANK_BY_52W_HIGH=True` to constants as the new default.
component: quant/signal_engine.py + quant/backtester.py + quant/constants.py + quant/run.py + quant/run_quant.py + quant/run_pipeline.py + quant/test_quant.py

ranking_scope.new: only breakout_long subsequence re-ranked by pct_from_52w_high, non-breakouts unchanged

delta_metrics.note: This ranking rule improved the primary north-star metric materially while leaving the two secondary bull windows and the stress window unchanged in the tested sample. That is exactly the desired shape for a slot-allocation change: better opportunity selection, no broad instability.

llm_metrics.note: No LLM scope change. This is a pure slot-allocation refinement inside the quant layer.

next_retry_requires: If extending allocation logic, keep the next experiment equally narrow: one additional breakout ranking key or one capital-allocation weight change, not both together
Do not revisit broad cross-strategy demotions like exp-20260419-002; this experiment worked precisely because it preserved non-breakout ordering
After more forward data accumulates, check whether the same slot-ranking advantage persists in live candidate sets before layering on breakout-specific risk weighting
notes: The experiment was intentionally designed to avoid the failure mode from exp-20260419-002. Instead of demoting an entire strategy relative to all other signals, it only improved ordering inside the breakout bucket. This keeps causality clean and minimizes unintended displacement of trend_long signals.

hypothesis: Even after breakout-quality filtering and slot-ranking improvements, breakout_long may still be structurally more fragile than trend_long. Reducing breakout_long risk budget to 0.75x or 0.5x of the base per-trade risk could improve expected_value_score by lowering the cost of breakout variance without changing signal selection.
change_summary: Added a backtester-only experiment path that allowed a configurable `BREAKOUT_RISK_MULT` during signal sizing, then compared `1.0`, `0.75`, and `0.5` across the current accepted A+B baseline (regime-aware exit + breakout quality gate + breakout slot ranking). The code was rolled back immediately after Gate 4 failure.

delta_metrics.note: Both lower-risk variants reduced drawdown modestly, but they also reduced expected_value_score in the primary window and all three non-overlapping windows. The breakout selection improvements already removed enough bad breakout exposure that further blunt risk cuts mostly shrink good breakout profits.

rejection_reason: Gate 4 failed. Lower breakout risk improved some secondary defensive metrics, but the north-star metric deteriorated in every tested window. This means the current problem is no longer 'breakout gets too much capital' in aggregate; after the accepted quality/ranking changes, the remaining breakout exposure is valuable enough that cutting size is net harmful.
next_retry_requires: Do not revisit broad breakout risk haircut without new evidence that breakout variance has worsened again in forward data
Prefer the next experiment to improve breakout selection or breakout-specific exit handling, not to bluntly reduce all breakout sizing
If returning to capital allocation later, test something more selective than a global breakout haircut (for example, only lower size on lower-ranked breakouts)
notes: Implementation was rolled back after the failed experiment. This rejection is about alpha direction, not about code quality.

hypothesis: Within the accepted breakout slot-ranking framework, `above_200ma` might add incremental information as a secondary ordering signal. If two breakout candidates are otherwise similar, preferring `above_200ma=True` could improve opportunity selection without changing the global ranking structure.
change_summary: Added a backtester-only experiment flag that let `rank_signals_for_allocation()` use `above_200ma` as a tie-breaker after `pct_from_52w_high`, then compared the current accepted baseline against the tie-break-enabled variant across the primary window and 3 non-overlapping windows. The implementation was fully rolled back after producing a null result.
component: quant/signal_engine.py + quant/backtester.py + quant/constants.py + quant/run.py + quant/run_quant.py + quant/run_pipeline.py + quant/test_quant.py

delta_metrics.note: The tie-breaker produced zero change in every tested window. In this sample, `above_200ma` did not add incremental allocation information beyond the already-accepted `pct_from_52w_high` breakout ranking and quality filter.

rejection_reason: Rejected for null effect. The experiment added no measurable value once the current breakout filter/ranking stack was already in place. That means `above_200ma` is either redundant with the existing breakout quality logic in this sample or too weak to affect scarce-slot ordering.
next_retry_requires: Do not revisit `above_200ma` as a pure breakout tie-breaker without new evidence that it changes actual slot collisions
Prefer the next ranking experiment to test a genuinely different signal such as `trade_quality_score` or a more selective breakout-specific exit path
Keep future breakout allocation experiments single-variable and backtester-only first, since the current ranking stack is already fairly optimized
notes: Implementation was rolled back immediately after the null result. This failed experiment was still valuable because it showed the next increment must come from a non-redundant breakout feature, not from repackaging an already-embedded trend-quality concept.

hypothesis: After breakout slot ranking improved EV, trend_long may still be leaving alpha on the table because same-day trend candidates are not explicitly ordered by trend strength. Re-ranking only the `trend_long` subsequence by `momentum_10d_pct` first and `pct_from_52w_high` second could improve slot selection without disturbing breakout ordering, exits, or strategy enablement.
change_summary: Added an experiment-only flag (`TREND_RANK_BY_MOMENTUM_AND_52W_HIGH`) so `rank_signals_for_allocation()` could re-order only the `trend_long` subsequence by `momentum_10d_pct desc`, then `pct_from_52w_high desc`, then confidence. The flag was wired through backtester and production entry points for parity, evaluated across three non-overlapping windows, then rolled back after Gate 4 failure.
component: quant/signal_engine.py + quant/backtester.py + quant/constants.py + quant/run.py + quant/run_quant.py + quant/run_pipeline.py + quant/test_quant.py

ranking_scope.unchanged: trend_long subsequence only; breakout_long ranking left intact

market_regime_summary.window_2: mixed to weaker tape with lower-quality trend follow-through

delta_metrics.note: The experiment had no effect in the primary window or the ripping-bull secondary window, which implies same-day trend slot collisions were either rare or already resolved by the existing post-dedup order. In the weaker 2024-10 to 2025-04 window it made things worse, suggesting the extra momentum preference mostly reweighted toward noisier late-move trend candidates rather than improving selection.

llm_metrics.note: No LLM scope or prompt changes. This was a pure quant slot-ordering test.

rejection_reason: Gate 4 failed. The north-star metric did not improve in any tested window, and one non-overlapping window regressed materially on both EV and sharpe_daily. A ranking rule that cannot improve the primary window and hurts another window is not worth promoting into the default stack.
next_retry_requires: Do not revisit trend_long ranking with the same key set (`momentum_10d_pct`, `pct_from_52w_high`) unless new evidence shows materially more same-day trend slot collisions
If trend allocation is revisited later, test a genuinely different trend-quality proxy such as risk-adjusted momentum or post-enrichment `trade_quality_score`, not another raw-momentum permutation
Prefer the next alpha search to move away from slot-ranking and toward a different mechanism class, since breakout ranking worked but trend ranking did not transfer
notes: Implementation was rolled back immediately after the failed experiment. This was still useful: it falsified the naive symmetry assumption that 'what worked for breakout ranking should also work for trend ranking'.

hypothesis: Scheduled daily alpha optimization pass: structured discovery, dedup, critical evaluation, diff analysis, and failure log update for 2026-04-19 session results.
change_summary: Produced docs/daily_alpha_optimization_20260419.md (structured 5-step alpha research report). Updated docs/alpha-optimization-playbook.md Section 6 to reflect resolved hypotheses and new priority ordering. No strategy code was modified (read-only analysis pass).

resolved_hypotheses: A7 direction terminated (global risk haircut invalid)

before_metrics.note: day-start with C strategy enabled and partial data

delta_metrics.note: EV delta +190.2% from day-start; all from accepted experiments exp-003 through exp-008

notes: This is a research/documentation record, not a strategy code experiment. The five steps (Discovery, Dedup, Evaluation, Diff, Failure Log) were executed autonomously against the full repository state. Recommended next experiment: NEW-A1 (trend_long momentum ranking), single causal variable, all data available.

hypothesis: If the entry-day regime-aware exit already helps, a slow in-trade regime refresh might further improve expected_value_score by retargeting open winners using only prior-day observable SPY/QQQ state, without changing entries, sizing, or LLM behavior.
change_summary: Prototyped an in-trade regime-update path for fixed ATR targets, then rolled it back after failing to obtain trustworthy before/after backtests in this environment. Kept only a minimal backtester measurement fix that redirects yfinance cache into a writable temp directory so the true blocker is surfaced cleanly.

market_regime_summary.primary: intended same multi-window protocol as exp-20260419-004
market_regime_summary.actual: experiment blocked before trustworthy multi-window evaluation could run

delta_metrics.note: No trustworthy delta. First blocker was yfinance sqlite cache failure (`unable to open database file` / `disk I/O error`). After redirecting cache to a writable temp directory, the environment still could not fetch OHLCV because requests were being routed to `127.0.0.1:9` and failed with curl connection errors. Under AGENTS Gate 4, this means the alpha experiment is unmeasurable and must not be retained.

rejection_reason: Blocked by measurement infrastructure, not by strategy evidence. The environment could not supply trustworthy OHLCV for the paired backtests, so the in-trade exit change was rolled back instead of being guessed into the codebase.
next_retry_requires: Restore reliable OHLCV access for backtester.py, either by fixing the proxy/network path or by introducing a local historical price cache inside the repository
Re-run the experiment on the same 3 non-overlapping windows with paired before/after metrics in one trusted data environment
Only reintroduce the in-trade regime-update code after Gate 4 evidence exists
notes: This failure is still useful. It proves the next blocking measurement problem is not LLM and not strategy logic; it is historical price availability inside the current execution environment. The strategy code path for in-trade target updates was fully rolled back. Only the temp-cache redirection remains because it reduces one local yfinance failure mode and exposes the true network blocker faster.

hypothesis: The current alpha loop is blocked by environment-level market-data failures, not by strategy logic. If backtester.py strips the broken localhost proxy injection (`127.0.0.1:9`) and forces yfinance's sqlite cache into a known writable temp directory, historical OHLCV downloads should become stable again without changing any signal or risk logic.
change_summary: Added a backtester-only measurement repair that removes broken proxy environment variables pointing to `127.0.0.1:9` before any yfinance calls and redirects yfinance cache files to the system temp directory. Added a targeted test to ensure the proxy sanitizer remains in place.

yfinance_failure_modes: OperationalError unable to open database file

delta_metrics.note: This is not a new alpha experiment, so before/after metrics describe blocked vs runnable state. The important change is that the same accepted strategy stack can once again be measured credibly in the current session.

next_retry_requires: If other repo entry points also rely on yfinance, consider applying the same proxy/cache guard there to avoid environment-specific failures outside the backtester
Resume paired alpha experiments now that the measurement path is restored
notes: Validated by a real end-to-end backtest run after the fix, plus a targeted unit test for the proxy sanitizer. No strategy logic, LLM behavior, or ranking/filter settings were changed.

hypothesis: If `regime-aware exit` already improves entry-day target width, extending the same regime mapping into open `trend_long` positions may further improve expected_value_score by retargeting winners using only prior-day observable SPY/QQQ state. Scope was intentionally narrow: no entry changes, no ranking changes, no breakout changes, no LLM changes.
change_summary: Implemented an experiment-only `IN_TRADE_REGIME_UPDATE` path in the backtester that, for open `trend_long` winners held at least 2 trading days and up at least 1 ATR, recomputed the target from the prior day's regime profile. Ran paired same-data before/after backtests across the primary window and two non-overlapping secondary windows, then rolled the code back after a strict null result.

market_regime_summary.window_2: older mixed window with sparse current-universe signal coverage

delta_metrics.note: Strict null effect in all measurable windows. The added retargeting logic did not change trade count, win rate, return, drawdown, or EV at all. This implies the current entry-day regime-aware target mapping already captures the relevant information for the sampled trades, so carrying the same mapping forward in-trade adds no incremental edge.

rejection_reason: Gate 4 failed by null effect. This was not merely 'too small to matter'—the paired same-data run produced zero change in every measured primary/secondary metric. Keeping extra exit-state complexity with no measurable edge would violate the complexity discipline in AGENTS.md.
next_retry_requires: Do not revisit in-trade target refresh by simply reapplying the same regime score used at entry

If trend exit alpha is revisited, test a genuinely different mechanism class (for example, a selective trailing/profit-lock rule or a different hold-quality proxy), not another carry-forward of the current regime map
Prefer the next high-value effort to unlock blocked alpha directions: LLM soft ranking (P-LLM) or C strategy revival (P-ERN)
notes: Important measurement note from this run: `BacktestEngine(..., config={"REGIME_AWARE_EXIT": True})` reproduced the accepted 0.7531 baseline, while the plain CLI invocation can still print a lower 0.4641 stack if `--regime-aware-exit` is omitted. The alpha verdict here is based on paired same-data runs against the accepted stack, not on that CLI-default drift.

hypothesis: Because recent `trend_long` losses cluster in the first 0-5 trading days, a fast confirmation-based exit may improve EV by cutting trend trades that fail to show early follow-through. The tested mechanism was intentionally different from the rejected trailing-stop and in-trade-regime ideas: after N trading days, exit only if the trade has not reached a minimum best-excursion in R and is still below entry.
change_summary: Added an experiment-only backtester branch for `trend_no_confirm`, then screened six parameter pairs against the accepted A+B stack (`REGIME_AWARE_EXIT=True`) on the primary window. All code was rolled back immediately after the screen. No production pipeline, signal ranking, LLM logic, or sizing logic was changed.

trigger.trial: after N trading days, if best excursion < min_profit_R * initial_R and close < entry, exit at close

delta_metrics.note: The mechanism failed the primary-window pre-screen. Loose settings (3d/0.25R, 4d/0.25R) never fired and therefore added zero edge; tighter settings fired 2-3 times but materially reduced EV, return, and win rate while slightly worsening drawdown. Because no tested pair improved even the primary window, the experiment was stopped before spending additional runtime on multi-window validation.

rejection_reason: The mechanism produced either strict null effect or clear primary-window regression, so it did not justify a full Gate 4 multi-window run. This suggests the observed early trend losses are not fixed by a naive early-confirmation exit; the same rule also cuts too many valid trend recoveries or late-strength winners.
next_retry_requires: Do not revisit `trend_no_confirm` with the same 'best excursion by day N' framing unless new evidence shows missed early confirmation is a persistent causal failure mode
If trend exit alpha is revisited, prefer a different mechanism class such as hold-quality scoring or selectively using richer semantic/event context, not another fast confirmation stop
Given the current evidence, the highest-value remaining alpha directions are still LLM soft ranking (blocked by P-LLM coverage) and C strategy revival (blocked by P-ERN data quality)
notes: This was a deliberate single-variable alpha screen. The code path was rolled back immediately after the run, leaving the repository logic unchanged.

hypothesis: Within the accepted breakout allocation stack, post-enrichment `trade_quality_score` may contain more scarce-slot information than raw `pct_from_52w_high`. Re-ranking only the `breakout_long` subsequence by `trade_quality_score` first, then `pct_from_52w_high`, could improve EV without changing entry rules, filters, exits, or trend ordering.
change_summary: Added an experiment-only backtester flag that reranked only enriched `breakout_long` signals by `trade_quality_score`, then ran paired baseline-vs-trial comparisons against the accepted A+B stack (`REGIME_AWARE_EXIT=True`). The experiment code was rolled back immediately after measurement. No production pipeline, LLM boundary, or sizing rule was changed.

ranking_scope.unchanged: breakout_long subsequence only; non-breakout order preserved

delta_metrics.note: The entire primary-window uplift came from removing one losing breakout trade (`IWM` entered 2026-01-22, -$714). Every non-overlapping comparison window outside the full primary sample was a strict null. This is useful evidence that the current breakout ranking stack is already close to locally saturated; TQS re-ranking changes too few real slot collisions to justify promotion.

llm_metrics.note: No LLM prompt, role, or attribution changes. Pure quant allocation experiment.

rejection_reason: Gate 4 failed on stability, not on the primary window. Although the full-window EV improved materially, 5 non-overlapping comparison windows showed zero improvement, so the result is not a stable majority-window gain. Keeping extra allocation complexity for a one-trade sample benefit would violate the anti-overfit discipline in AGENTS.md.
next_retry_requires: Do not promote breakout TQS re-ranking as a default rule without new evidence of repeated same-day breakout slot collisions
If allocation is revisited, require a collision-attribution view that shows which lower-ranked breakout displaced which higher-ranked one before changing ranking again
Prefer the next alpha search to move to a different mechanism class or a genuinely richer context source, because the current breakout ranking stack appears near local saturation
notes: Experiment code was fully rolled back after measurement; repository trading logic remains unchanged.

hypothesis: `trend_long` may still be leaving money on the table because it shares the same entry-day regime-aware ATR target width with breakout trades. A small trend-only target widening could improve EV without touching entry selection, filters, ranking, sizing, or LLM logic.
change_summary: Added an experiment-only backtester knob to widen only `trend_long` target multipliers after the accepted `REGIME_AWARE_EXIT` profile was applied, then ran paired same-data comparisons against the accepted A+B stack. Primary-window pre-screen favored small bonuses (`+0.25`, `+0.5` ATR), but the code was rolled back after multi-window validation showed regime-fragile results.

delta_metrics.note: The idea improves the accepted primary window, but the effect is not stable: one large comparison window (`2025-04-21 -> 2025-10-17`) and one key subwindow (`2025-12-20 -> 2026-02-17`) both regress materially, while the positive windows are smaller or near-zero. This is not a robust 'trend target should just be wider' conclusion; it is regime-fragile target stretching.

rejection_reason: Rejected on multi-window stability despite passing the primary-window screen. A blanket trend-only target widening improved one main sample but regressed a meaningful continuation window and did not deliver a clean majority of economically meaningful gains. Keeping a global wider-target rule would add complexity around a regime-fragile effect and risks repeating the same 'single-window exit tuning' failure mode.
next_retry_requires: Do not revisit `trend_long` target widening as a blanket multiplier bonus without a selector that explains when wider targets help and when they hurt
If trend exit alpha is revisited, prefer a conditional hold-quality mechanism or richer context signal rather than another global target-width permutation
Current highest-value alpha directions remain `LLM soft ranking` (blocked by P-LLM coverage) and `C strategy revival` (blocked by P-ERN data quality)
notes: Experiment code was fully rolled back after measurement; repository trading logic remains unchanged. The accepted stack at the end of the run is still the same A+B baseline with `REGIME_AWARE_EXIT=True` and accepted breakout ranking.

hypothesis: The highest-value remaining alpha direction is `LLM soft ranking`, but it is blocked because the automatic LLM pipeline writes `investment_advice_YYYYMMDD.json` without also creating `llm_prompt_resp_YYYYMMDD.json`. Centralizing replay-log mirroring inside `llm_advisor.save_advice()` should unblock future P-LLM coverage accumulation without changing any trading rule, filter, sizing, or backtest metric.
change_summary: Added automatic replay-log mirroring to `llm_advisor.save_advice()`: when saving a real dated `investment_advice_YYYYMMDD.json`, it now also writes `llm_prompt_resp_YYYYMMDD.json` unless that replay file already exists. Added targeted tests for auto-mirroring, non-overwrite behavior, and fake-response skip behavior. No strategy logic or backtester decision path changed.

notes: This run intentionally did not propose another unblocked A/B alpha tweak because recent history already falsified the remaining low-complexity variants, while the two highest-value alpha directions are still structurally blocked by a non-running daily pipeline.

hypothesis: `trend_long` exit alpha may still exist, but not as a blanket wider target. If only the highest-quality trend entries (`confidence_score == 1.0`) receive a modest target bonus, the system may capture more continuation upside without repeating the rejected global target-widening idea.
change_summary: Added an experiment-only backtester branch that widened targets only for `trend_long` signals with perfect entry confidence, then screened `+0.25`, `+0.5`, and `+1.0 ATR` against the accepted A+B stack (`REGIME_AWARE_EXIT=True`). The code was fully rolled back immediately after measurement. Primary-window screen improved at `+0.25` and `+0.5`, but multi-window validation rejected the mechanism.

delta_metrics.note: The mechanism improved the accepted primary window but failed the majority-window stability check. The same selective widening that helped in the main sample materially regressed the large ripping-bull comparison window and the key continuation subwindow. This is not stable enough to justify promotion.

rejection_reason: Rejected on Gate 4 multi-window stability. Conditioning the target bonus on perfect entry quality did not solve the fragility seen in the earlier blanket trend-widening experiment; the uplift remained sample-dependent rather than robust.
next_retry_requires: Do not retry static entry-quality-conditioned trend target widening without new evidence that entry-time signal quality predicts hold-time continuation better than the current exit stack
If trend exit alpha is revisited, prefer richer in-trade hold-quality context or semantic/event context rather than another target-width mutation keyed only off entry metadata
Practical priority still favors `LLM soft ranking` once replay coverage grows and `C strategy revival` once earnings snapshots accumulate

If the runtime environment still cannot import `yfinance`, then this blocker remains the real reason alpha_search cannot proceed.
